rationale: All four round-2 falsifiability asks are addressed in the rewritten Verification commands. Bead 3 now guards both new test selectors with explicit function-existence checks and PASS-line greps, so missing tests cannot false-pass through go test's no-match behavior. Bead 2 now separately proves the codex symlink refusal test exists/runs and adds one PASS-line Verification for each per-agent full-equality managed-doc test. Bead 4's guardrail check extracts the Bead-loop guardrails section body, requires it non-empty, and greps fences only inside that body. I also parsed work_chunks and found no duplicate key_file_paths across beads. The plan is gate-forward and falsifiable enough for plan approval.

findings.issue: B3 Verification commands now include anchored function-existence checks plus PASS-line greps for TestRun_CompletePerfPairSubprocessBudget and TestFetchChildren, closing the no-tests-to-run false-pass mode.
B2 now verifies TestRunCodex_RefusesSymlinkedAGENTSmd specifically, with a function-existence guard and a matching PASS-line grep.
B2 now has explicit per-agent full-equality managed-doc Verifications for Claude, Codex, and Copilot, each using the function-existence plus PASS-line pattern.
B4 fence checking is scoped to the extracted Bead-loop guardrails section body and requires that section body to be non-empty.
Parsed work_chunks key_file_paths show no duplicate touched paths across the four beads; the bead-unique doc-target fix holds.

Every newly named test in the plan uses the required PASS-line pattern: TestRunCodex_RefusesSymlinkedAGENTSmd, TestManagedDocContent_Claude, TestManagedDocContent_Codex, TestManagedDocContent_Copilot, TestRun_CompletePerfPairSubprocessBudget, and TestFetchChildren.
